body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Reasoning

long-agentic-session-stress is a compressed proxy for a real long session: several plain user prompts in one live session, several eliciting tool calls, stress-testing that irrlicht's transcript parser stays coherent across a large volume of interleaved assistant fragments and tool-call/result pairs. The spec asserts (a) state stays continuously `working` WITHIN a turn — no flicker — (b) clean `working → ready` / `ready → working` BETWEEN turns, (c) state never wedged, and (d) the final state is `ready`. The `requires` are `headless_mode` + `tool_calls`; the **Primitive exercised** is `tool_calls` + `headless_mode`, which map to `capabilities.json` keys `tool_calls: true` and `headless_mode: true`.

**agent_supports: yes.** aider's REPL is natively multi-turn and runs tools inline. One process holds the session open and appends every prompt + reply to the same per-cwd `.aider.chat.history.md`; under `--yes-always` aider auto-accepts file-add and shell prompts so tool-call turns don't stall at a permission gate (unlike the modern claudecode default that forced the claudecode sibling to drop tool calls). `capabilities.json` records `tui: true`, `tool_calls: true`, `git_integration: true`; aider emits `> Applied edit to <file>` (file-edit tool) and `> Running <cmd>` (shell tool) blockquotes that the parser recognises (parser.go `appliedEditRE` / `runningRE`). The committed `drive-aider-interactive.sh` tmux driver chains many send+wait_turn cycles in one live REPL, several eliciting tool calls — so a multi-turn, tool-bearing session is directly drivable. The `yes-always-multi-step` and `full-lifecycle-toolcall` regression captures already demonstrate aider running multiple tool steps in one invocation.

**irrlicht_observes: partial.** Two distinct sub-claims, with opposite robustness:

*Within-turn no-flicker (robust).* The aider adapter watches `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` — markdown, not JSONL. The parser opens a turn on each `#### ` user line and, within that turn, emits `assistant_message` at every `> Tokens:` line and `tool_result` (open+close coalesced) at every `> Applied edit`/`> Running` line — but deliberately NOT `turn_done`, because under `--yes-always` aider may re-prompt the model within one turn. None of these intermediate event kinds is terminal in the state classifier: only `turn_done` with no open tool and no trailing question flips `working → ready` (state_classifier.go; the classifier test pins `assistant_message, no open tools` as NOT done). So the within-turn invariant — state stays continuously `working` across many interleaved assistant fragments and tool-call/result pairs — holds robustly, which is exactly the parser-coherence property this scenario stress-tests.

*Between-turn arc (fragile).* End-of-turn is synthesized OUT-OF-BAND by `IdleFlush` only after the transcript is quiet for `aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter` (1500ms), because aider's idle TUI prompt never lands in the markdown history. The daemon's `SessionDetector` polls working sessions every `staleWorkingRefreshInterval` (5s), so the effective `working → ready` latency after a turn's last line is ~5s. When each prompt is followed by ≥~5s idle, IdleFlush fires and the daemon observes a full per-turn `working → ready` arc. But `TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking` pins the failure mode: a `####` arriving while the turn is still open (next prompt lands before IdleFlush) opens the new turn WITHOUT an intervening `turn_done`, so the session stays `working` across that boundary and the per-turn reset is lost. The captured `multi-turn-conversation` regression run shows exactly this — one `ready → working` and no intermediate `working → ready` flips before `process_exited`.

## Why PARTIAL (and the long-session compounding)

The matrix note is precise: "idle-flush windows compound on long sessions." Each of the N turn boundaries independently depends on the next `####` not outrunning the 1.5s idle window + the 5s poll. Over a single turn (basic-turn) the odds of a blur are low; over 4 stress-scenario turns the odds that AT LEAST ONE boundary blurs compound, so the per-turn `ready → working → ready` topology the verify block asserts (`transitions_topology` with four arcs) is only reliably observable when the recipe inserts ≥~5s idle (3s sleep + wait_turn settle) after every prompt. The final `ready` state is reached regardless (IdleFlush always fires once the session goes quiet at teardown), and `has_waiting_state: false` holds because plain prompts never trip the question/imperative waiting cue. The within-turn `contains_tool_call: true` assertion is fully observed via the `tool_result` events. The arc COUNT between turns is the only fragile assertion — hence `partial`, not `yes`.

This ratifies the existing matrix row (aider/long-agentic-session-stress: yes/partial, "idle-flush windows compound on long sessions") — no drift.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: aider writes no in-band turn-end marker, so each between-turn working→ready flip is synthesized only by IdleFlush after ~1.5s quiet + the 5s detector poll (~5s effective latency). The per-turn arcs are observable only when every prompt is followed by ≥~5s idle (canonical recipe's 3s sleep + wait_turn settle).

Idle-flush windows compound on long sessions: each of the N turn boundaries independently needs the next #### prompt to NOT outrun the idle window. If any prompt lands before IdleFlush fires, that boundary blurs (TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking) and the session stays working across it — losing one of the expected per-turn resets. More turns = higher cumulative blur risk than basic-turn.

The within-turn no-flicker invariant is robust, NOT fragile: assistant_message (per `> Tokens:` line) and tool_result (per `> Applied edit`/`> Running` line) are non-terminal in the state classifier, so state stays continuously working across many interleaved fragments and tool calls within one turn. The fragility is purely at the between-turn boundary.
cost_tracking: false — per-turn Contribution carries token counts (sent/received) from each `> Tokens:` line but provider cost is absent unless aider prints a `, $… message` suffix.

Exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, tool-result content, and sub-second timing vary run-to-run (model-output variance); only the structural multi-turn arc, the within-turn no-flicker property, single-session count, and final ready state are stable across re-records.

sources.note: FilesUnderCWD{Filename: .aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}; per-process Parser instance tracking idle-flush state; CommandPattern /aider — markdown transcript, not JSONL.

Turn opens on `#### `; `> Tokens:` emits assistant_message (NOT turn_done) carrying a per-call Tokens snapshot; `> Applied edit`/`> Running` emit tool_result (open+close coalesced under --yes-always); turn-end synthesized via IdleFlush after aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms); comment documents ~5s effective working→ready latency.

TestParser_ToolCall_AppliedEdit / TestParser_ToolCall_RunningShell (tool_result events); TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn (assistant_message per turn, no in-band turn_done); TestParser_IdleFlush_AfterMultipleModelCalls_EmitsTurnDone (idle synthesizes turn_done); TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking (fast next prompt → no per-turn reset, the compounding fragility).

Only turn_done (no open tool, no trailing question) flips working→ready; assistant_message and tool_result are non-terminal — this is what makes the within-turn no-flicker invariant robust across many interleaved fragments and tool calls.
staleWorkingRefreshInterval = 5 * time.Second — the working-session poll cadence that sets the ~5s effective working→ready latency for idle-flush turn-end and underlies the per-turn arc fragility.

tool_calls: true, headless_mode: true, tui: true, git_integration: true, streaming_output: true — the two requires/primitive keys for this scenario are both true; cost_tracking: false, structured_output_jsonl: false (markdown transcript).

tmux REPL driver; wait_turn blocks on a new `> Tokens:` line; keeps one aider session alive across all send/wait_turn cycles — basis for agent_supports: yes (native multi-turn REPL with inline tools under --yes-always).

Captured daemon events: single ready→working, no intermediate working→ready flips before terminal process_exited — direct evidence of the cadence-fragility / idle-flush-compounding caveat at the between-turn boundary.
